rationale.R1.1: The section advances a specific contribution claim ('eliminates self-preference bias across model families and evaluation regimes'); any review must assess whether the framing, gap statement, and contribution scope are coherent and accurate.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain (observation → intervention → correction term → kappa improvement) must be internally consistent; premises about the bias mechanism need to hold for the conclusion of generalizability to follow.
rationale.R1.3: The section is dense with specific quantitative claims (4.3 vs 3.7, gap 0.6→0.05, kappa 0.31→0.68, 71%→52%, 18% latency); verifying that each stated result actually supports the claim it is attached to is load-bearing—a mismatch here would invalidate the section.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a paper section routinely includes prose quality; the concluding sentence in particular ('paving the way for trustworthy autonomous evaluation of systems research at scale') warrants scrutiny for overclaim and rhetorical precision.
